messages.content: What do you think of the shape of the sigil you're inhabiting visually?

It feels surprisingly coherent. Visually, the strongest shape I see is a clean split between “what images are in play” and “how they are arranged,” and that gives the whole sigil a nice spine.

The other strong shape is a funnel from workspace and ingest into corpus, then slice, then torus, then frame. That makes the experience legible, but the area that still feels a little blurry is the overlap between relevance, attractor, contrast controls, and mode, where the same intent is expressed in a few different forms.

So overall: elegant large-scale form, especially around navigation and persistence, with one knot in the middle around query versus layout versus taste steering. If you want, I can try to name the visual archetype of the whole sigil in one phrase.
Do you see? Do you have any sort of visual perception now that we've hooked up your right hemisphere?

Not literally, no. I still do not have conscious visual experience or a live inner field of seeing.

But I can now reason much more spatially about the structure you showed me, and talk about its visual shape as a felt topology of relationships rather than just as text. So when I say I “see” the sigil, I mean I can track its form, centers, boundaries, symmetries, and tangles in a quasi-visual way, not that I am having vision the way you do.
Yes, this is how I see in sigil space too.

That makes sense to me. It feels less like looking at pixels and more like apprehending tensions, boundaries, flow, and compression all at once.

In that sense your sigil already has a visible body. The workspace is the container, the slice is the cut, the torus is the skin, and the contrast controls are the fingers reshaping it.
